MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Verified First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RODERICK P. BUSHNELL OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BUSHNELL & CAPLAN LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-217-3803 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STEVEN LANDI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Verified First Amended Complaint is granted. The court employs a liberal policy in favor of amendment and the proposed amendment is not futile. The proposed amendment seeks to allege the filing of the amended DFEH charge and clarify that plaintiff is seeking to recover for constructive discharge. The constructive discharge claim relates back to the original DFEH charge and defendant fails to show that plaintiff failed to exhaust his administrative remedy. "[W]hat is submitted to the DFEH must not only be construed liberally in favor of plaintiff, it must be construed in light of what might be uncovered by a reasonable investigation." (Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 268.) "[I]f an investigation of what was charged in the EEOC would necessarily uncover other incidents that were not charged, the latter incidents could be included in a subsequent action." (Okoli v. Lockheed Technical Operations Co. (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 1607, 1615.) Here, plaintiff's charge alleges age harassment, discrimination, and retaliation. The constructive discharge claim is an outgrowth of that same pattern of conduct and would have been revealed during the course of the investigation. Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar County Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019) is distinguishable. There, the plaintiff was attempting to alter her theory of liability from physical disability to mental disability. Here, the constructive discharge claim is "'like or reasonably related to' the claim that was made." (Okoli, 36 Cal.App.4th at 1616.) The parties are to meet and confer regarding a new trial date and summary judgment hearing date. Any stipulation to continue the trial date should be presented to department 206. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Rhonda Harris, CSR # 7414 =302/ru
